name: Greek yogurt with berries and honey
steps: Spoon the Greek yogurt into a bowl.
Top with the mixed berries and a sprinkle of chia seeds.
Drizzle with honey and serve.

name: Veggie omelette with spinach and feta
steps: Beat the eggs with salt and pepper.
Melt butter in a nonstick pan, wilt the spinach briefly, then pour in the eggs.
Scatter feta over the top, fold the omelette when nearly set, and serve.

name: Overnight oats with peanut butter and banana
steps: Stir the oats, plant milk, peanut butter and chia seeds together in a jar.
Refrigerate overnight.
Top with sliced banana in the morning and serve.

name: Avocado toast with poached egg
steps: Toast the bread and poach the egg in simmering water for about 3 minutes.
Mash the avocado with lemon juice and salt and spread on the toast.
Top with the poached egg and a pinch of red pepper flakes.

name: Smoothie bowl with chia and granola
steps: Blend the frozen berries, banana and plant milk into a thick smoothie.
Pour into a bowl.
Top with granola and chia seeds and serve right away.

name: Cottage cheese with pineapple
steps: Spoon the cottage cheese into a bowl.
Top with pineapple chunks and serve.

name: Grilled chicken Caesar salad
steps: Season and grill the chicken breast until cooked through, then slice.
Toss the chopped romaine with Caesar dressing and parmesan.
Top with the sliced chicken and a grind of black pepper.

name: Quinoa and roasted veggie bowl
steps: Cook the quinoa in salted water until fluffy.
Toss the broccoli, diced sweet potato and chickpeas in olive oil and roast at 425 F until tender.
Pile everything in a bowl and drizzle with tahini.

name: Turkey and hummus wrap
steps: Spread the hummus over the tortilla.
Layer with turkey, spinach and sliced tomato.
Roll up tightly and slice in half.

name: Lentil soup with whole-grain bread
steps: Saute the onion, carrot, celery and garlic in olive oil until soft.
Add the lentils, cumin and broth and bring to a boil.
Simmer about 30 minutes until the lentils are tender, then serve with crusty bread.

name: Tuna poke bowl
steps: Cube the tuna and toss with soy sauce and sesame oil.
Spoon rice into a bowl and arrange edamame, sliced cucumber and avocado on top.
Add the marinated tuna and serve.

name: Caprese and grilled chicken plate
steps: Grill the seasoned chicken breast until cooked through, then slice.
Layer sliced tomato, mozzarella and basil on a plate.
Add the chicken, drizzle with olive oil and balsamic, and season.

name: Black bean and sweet potato tacos
steps: Toss diced sweet potato in olive oil and cumin and roast at 425 F until tender.
Warm the black beans and the tortillas.
Fill the tortillas with sweet potato and beans, top with red onion and a squeeze of lime.

name: Baked salmon with asparagus
steps: Heat the oven to 400 F and line a sheet pan.
Arrange the salmon and asparagus, drizzle with olive oil, garlic, salt and lemon.
Roast 12 to 15 minutes until the salmon flakes easily.

name: Stir-fried tofu and broccoli with brown rice
steps: Press and cube the tofu, then pan-fry until golden.
Stir-fry the broccoli with garlic and ginger, then return the tofu and add soy sauce.
Toss in sesame oil and serve over brown rice.

name: Grilled steak with sweet potato
steps: Bake the sweet potato at 400 F until soft, about 45 minutes.
Season the steak with salt, pepper and garlic powder.
Sear in a hot pan or grill 3 to 4 minutes per side, rest, then slice and serve with the sweet potato.

name: Shrimp and zucchini noodles
steps: Spiralize the zucchini into noodles.
Saute the shrimp with garlic in olive oil until pink, then remove.
Toss the zucchini noodles in the pan briefly, return the shrimp, and finish with lemon and red pepper flakes.

name: Chicken and veggie curry with rice
steps: Brown the chicken with onion and garlic in a pot.
Stir in the curry powder, then add the vegetables and coconut milk.
Simmer 20 minutes until the chicken is cooked and the sauce thickens, and serve over rice.

name: Eggplant parmesan with side salad
steps: Slice the eggplant, dip in beaten egg and breadcrumbs, and bake at 400 F until golden.
Layer the eggplant with marinara and cheeses in a baking dish.
Bake until bubbly and serve with a simple side salad.

name: Turkey chili
steps: Brown the ground turkey with onion, bell pepper and garlic.
Stir in the chili powder, beans and tomatoes.
Simmer 30 to 40 minutes, adjust seasoning, and serve.

name: Miso-glazed cod with bok choy
steps: Whisk the miso and mirin and brush over the cod, then broil 8 to 10 minutes until glazed and flaky.
Stir-fry the bok choy with garlic in sesame oil until just tender.
Serve the cod over the bok choy.

name: Apple slices with almond butter
steps: Slice the apple.
Serve with almond butter for dipping.

name: Handful of mixed nuts
steps: Measure out a small handful of mixed nuts and enjoy.

name: Carrot sticks and hummus
steps: Cut the carrots into sticks.
Serve with hummus.

name: Protein shake
steps: Blend the protein powder, milk and ice until smooth.
Pour and serve.

name: Edamame with sea salt
steps: Steam or boil the edamame for 5 minutes.
Sprinkle with sea salt and serve.

name: Dark chocolate and strawberries
steps: Arrange the strawberries on a plate.
Serve with squares of dark chocolate.

name: Beef Birria (Birria de res estilo Jalisco)
steps: Devein the guajillo, ancho and cascabel chiles, toast on a comal and simmer in water until soft.
Blend the chiles with onion, garlic, vinegar, cumin, oregano, cinnamon and cloves until smooth.
Coat the beef in the adobo and marinate refrigerated at least 5 hours or overnight.
Cook the meat with bay leaves and water in a covered pot 3 to 4 hours until it shreds easily.
Blend the tomatoes into the broth, simmer 15 minutes, and serve with onion, cilantro, lime and warm tortillas.

name: Drowned Sandwich (Torta ahogada de Guadalajara)
steps: Boil the tomatoes, then blend most with garlic, half an onion, cumin, cloves and oregano; simmer 5 minutes for the mild sauce.
Blend the remaining tomatoes with the chiles de arbol, garlic and onion for the spicy sauce.
Pickle sliced onion in lime juice, vinegar and salt.
Split each birote, scoop out some crumb, and fill with refried beans and carnitas.
Drown the tortas in the tomato sauce, add the spicy chile de arbol sauce to taste and top with pickled onion.

name: Beef in Its Juices (Carne en su jugo estilo Jalisco)
steps: Blend tomatillos, onion, garlic, cilantro, serrano chiles and cumin with a cup of stock until smooth.
Finely slice the beef into small pieces and season with cumin, salt and pepper.
Crisp the chopped bacon in a pot, remove and reserve, then sear the beef in the bacon fat.
Add the tomatillo sauce, the bacon and the rest of the stock; simmer covered about 1 hour.
Serve over the pinto beans, topped with chopped onion, cilantro, radishes, lime and warm tortillas.

name: Red Pozole (Pozole rojo estilo Jalisco)
steps: Simmer the pork shoulder and neck bones with onion, garlic, bay leaves and salt until tender; reserve the broth.
Add the drained hominy to the pork broth and cook about 40 minutes until the kernels bloom open.
Soak the guajillo, ancho and arbol chiles, then blend with roasted tomatoes, garlic and oregano and strain into the pot.
Return the shredded pork to the pot and simmer 15 minutes, adjusting salt.
Serve topped with shredded lettuce, onion, radishes, oregano and lime, with tostadas on the side.

name: Colima Tatemado (Tatemado de Colima)
steps: Devein and toast the guajillo, pasilla and ancho chiles, then soak until soft.
Blend the chiles with vinegar, garlic, thyme, cumin, oregano and salt into a dense adobo and strain.
Coat the pork in the adobo, top with bay leaves and sliced ginger, and marinate refrigerated at least 12 hours.
Cover and bake at low heat (about 120 to 150 C) for 3 hours until the meat is very soft and saucy.
Serve brothy, eaten with a spoon, alongside refried beans, red onion in lime and tortillas.

name: Colima Sopitos (Sopitos colimenses)
steps: Boil the ground pork in salted water with garlic and bay leaves, then crumble it finely, reserving the broth.
Blend cooked tomatoes with garlic, oregano, cumin and a cup of the meat broth, then simmer the sauce about 10 minutes.
Mix masa harina with water, form small balls, flatten into thick discs and cook on a griddle, pinching the edges to make a rim.
Fry the masa discs in lard or oil until golden.
Layer each sopito with refried beans, the shredded pork, onion and cabbage, bathe in the warm tomato-oregano sauce and top with cotija.

name: Nikujaga (肉じゃが)
steps: Cut potatoes, carrot and onion into bite-size chunks. Heat oil in a pot and stir-fry the beef until it changes color.
Add potatoes, carrot, onion and shirataki and stir to coat, then pour in dashi and bring to a boil.
Skim off scum, add sugar, sake, mirin and soy sauce, and cover with a drop-lid.
Simmer over medium-low heat about 15 to 20 minutes until the potatoes are tender and the liquid has reduced.
Let it rest a few minutes so the flavors soak in, then serve.

name: Oyakodon (親子丼)
steps: Slice onion thinly and cut chicken into bite-size pieces. Beat the eggs loosely in a bowl.
In a small pan, combine dashi, soy sauce, mirin and sugar and bring to a simmer, then add the onion.
Add the chicken and simmer about 5 to 6 minutes until cooked through.
Pour about three-quarters of the egg over the surface, cover and cook 1 minute, then add the rest and cook 30 seconds until just set.
Slide over a bowl of hot rice and top with mitsuba or green onion.

name: Grilled Salted Mackerel (鯖の塩焼き)
steps: Score the skin of each mackerel fillet, sprinkle salt over both sides, and let sit about 10 minutes.
Pat off the moisture that has drawn out, then optionally brush lightly with sake.
Grill skin-side up under a preheated broiler about 6 to 7 minutes until golden and cooked through.
Serve with grated daikon and a lemon wedge.

name: Gyudon (牛丼)
steps: Cut onion into 1cm wedges and julienne the ginger.
In a pot combine water, sugar, soy sauce, sake, mirin, onion and ginger, and bring to a simmer.
Once boiling, add the beef, loosening the slices with chopsticks, and skim off the scum.
Simmer over medium-low heat about 20 minutes until the onion is soft and the sauce has reduced.
Spoon the beef and some broth over bowls of hot rice.

name: Japanese Beef Curry (カレー)
steps: Cut onion into wedges and chop potato and carrot into chunks. Brown the beef with the onion in oil until softened.
Add the carrot and potato, stir briefly, then pour in the water and bring to a boil.
Skim off scum and simmer over low heat about 20 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Turn off the heat, break in the curry roux and stir until dissolved, then simmer a few more minutes until thickened.
Serve ladled over hot rice.

name: Chawanmushi (茶碗蒸し)
steps: Cool the dashi, mix with salt and light soy sauce. Beat the eggs and stir in the dashi at a 1:3 egg-to-dashi ratio, then strain.
Blanch the chicken, slice the shiitake and kamaboko, and divide among cups with the egg mixture.
Steam over strong heat 2 to 3 minutes until steam rises steadily.
Vent the lid, then steam over low heat 23 to 25 minutes until set.
When a skewer in the center releases clear liquid, top with mitsuba and serve.

name: Doenjang-jjigae (된장찌개)
steps: Bring anchovy and kelp stock to a boil, then dissolve the doenjang into it.
Add potato and onion and simmer until starting to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add zucchini, tofu, garlic and gochugaru and simmer another 5 minutes.
Stir in sliced green chili and green onion, simmer 1 to 2 minutes, and serve hot with rice.

name: Kimchi-jjigae (김치찌개)
steps: Saute chopped pork and sliced kimchi with sesame oil over low heat for 3 to 5 minutes.
Add water, kimchi brine, garlic and gochugaru and bring to a boil.
Simmer covered for 20 to 25 minutes until the pork and kimchi are tender.
Add sliced tofu and onion, simmer 5 more minutes, top with green onion, and serve.

name: Bibimbap (비빔밥)
steps: Blanch spinach and soybean sprouts separately, then season each with sesame oil, garlic and salt.
Saute the carrot, zucchini and shiitake separately, and stir-fry the beef with soy sauce and garlic.
Mix gochujang with a little sesame oil and sugar to make the bibimbap sauce.
Mound rice in each bowl, arrange the seasoned vegetables and beef on top, add a fried egg, and serve with the sauce to mix in.

name: Bulgogi (불고기)
steps: Blend or grate the pear, onion, garlic and ginger and mix with soy sauce, sugar, sesame oil and black pepper.
Coat the thinly sliced beef in the marinade and rest at least 30 minutes.
Sear the beef in a hot pan or grill over high heat until caramelized and just cooked through.
Toss with sliced green onion and serve with rice.

name: Dak-bokkeum-tang (닭볶음탕)
steps: Parboil the chicken pieces briefly and drain to remove excess fat and impurities.
Mix gochugaru, gochujang, soy sauce, garlic, sugar and ginger into a sauce.
Combine chicken, sauce and water in a pot and simmer 15 minutes, then add potato and carrot.
Add onion and continue simmering until the chicken is tender and the sauce thickens, finishing with green onion.

name: Japchae (잡채)
steps: Soak the glass noodles, then boil until tender and toss with soy sauce and sesame oil.
Saute the onion, carrot, mushroom and seasoned beef separately, and blanch and season the spinach.
Combine the noodles, vegetables and beef in a large pan with soy sauce, sugar, garlic and sesame oil.
Toss over low heat until evenly mixed and glossy, finish with sesame seeds, and serve warm.

name: Mapo Tofu (麻婆豆腐)
steps: Cut tofu into 1 cm cubes and blanch in lightly salted boiling water, then drain gently.
Heat oil and fry the ground beef until crisp and browned, then push aside.
Add doubanjiang, mashed black beans, chili powder, garlic and ginger; fry until the oil turns red and fragrant.
Add a little water and soy sauce, slide in the tofu, and simmer briefly, thickening in additions of cornstarch slurry.
Finish on high heat, top with scallions and ground roasted Sichuan peppercorn, and serve hot.

name: Red-Braised Pork Belly (红烧肉)
steps: Cut pork belly into 3 cm cubes, soak briefly to remove blood, then drain and pat dry.
In a dry pan toast ginger, star anise, cinnamon and bay, then sear the pork until lightly browned.
Add Shaoxing wine, both soy sauces and rock sugar; stir to coat the meat in deep red color.
Pour in boiling water to just cover, skim the foam, cover, and braise on low heat about 1 hour.
Uncover, raise the heat and reduce until the sauce is thick and glossy; no extra salt is needed.

name: Century Egg and Pork Congee (皮蛋瘦肉粥)
steps: Rinse the rice, toss with a little oil and salt, then bring to a boil with water at about 10:1 and simmer 40 minutes into a smooth congee.
Marinate thinly sliced lean pork with salt, a little starch and water until silky.
Dice the century eggs and add with shredded ginger to the congee, simmering until the rice fully breaks down.
Stir the pork slices through the hot congee until just cooked and tender.
Season with salt and white pepper and finish with chopped scallions.

name: Tomato and Egg Stir-Fry (番茄炒蛋)
steps: Beat the eggs well with a splash of water until frothy.
Heat plenty of oil over medium-high and scramble the eggs until just set, then remove.
Stir-fry the tomato wedges until softened and saucy, adding a little sugar to balance the acidity.
Return the eggs, toss to combine, and add salt only at the end so the dish does not weep liquid.
Finish with chopped scallion and serve.

name: Cumin Lamb (孜然羊肉)
steps: Slice the lamb thin against the grain and marinate briefly with wine.
Sear the lamb in hot oil over high heat with ginger and scallion until it changes color and the edges crisp.
Add coarsely crushed cumin seeds and chili powder, tossing quickly so the spices toast and coat the meat.
Stir through sliced onion, garlic and sesame seeds, season with salt, and reduce any liquid.
Turn off the heat, fold in cilantro, and serve immediately.

name: Lion's Head Meatballs (狮子头)
steps: Hand-chop the pork to rice-grain size so it stays loose, then mix in diced water chestnut, ginger, scallion, egg and a little starch.
Whip the mixture and shape into large balls, throwing them between the hands to firm them up.
Brown the meatballs in oil, then transfer to a pot with water, soy sauce, wine, ginger, scallion and rock sugar.
Cover and braise gently over low heat for 2 to 3 hours until meltingly tender.
Lift out the meatballs, thicken a little braising liquid into a glaze, and pour it over to serve.

name: Louisiana Red Beans and Rice
steps: Soak the red beans overnight, then drain.
Simmer the ham hocks in stock with bay leaves and garlic for about 1 hour to build the base.
Saute the onion, bell pepper and celery (the holy trinity) in fat until soft, then add to the pot with the beans, sliced andouille and Creole seasoning.
Simmer uncovered 2 to 3 hours until the beans are creamy, mashing some against the pot to thicken.
Pull the ham from the hocks back into the pot and serve over hot white rice.

name: Creole Gumbo
steps: Make a dark roux by stirring the oil and flour constantly over medium heat 30 to 45 minutes until chocolate-colored.
Stir the onion, bell pepper, celery and garlic into the roux and cook until softened.
Add stock, tomatoes, browned andouille, chicken, okra and Creole seasoning, then simmer 1 hour.
Add the shrimp and cook 7 to 10 minutes until pink, then remove from heat and stir in file powder.
Serve over hot white rice.

name: Southern Smothered Chicken
steps: Season the chicken, dredge in seasoned flour, and pan-fry in oil until golden on all sides, then set aside.
Add butter and sliced onions to the pan and cook 8 to 10 minutes until softened and caramelized, then stir in the garlic.
Sprinkle in reserved flour and stir 2 to 3 minutes, then whisk in the chicken broth to form a smooth gravy.
Return the chicken to the pan, cover, and simmer 30 to 35 minutes until tender.
Serve over rice or mashed potatoes with the onion gravy spooned over.

name: Collard Greens with Smoked Turkey
steps: Simmer the smoked turkey in broth with onion and garlic for about 1 hour to build the pot likker.
Wash, stem and chop the collard greens.
Add the greens to the pot with vinegar and red pepper flakes, pushing them down into the liquid.
Simmer low and slow 2 to 3 hours until very tender, then pull the turkey meat from the bone back into the pot.
Season with salt and pepper and serve with the pot likker and cornbread.

name: Lowcountry Shrimp and Grits
steps: Cook the stone-ground grits low and slow in the milk and water until creamy, then stir in butter and cheddar.
Render the bacon and andouille in a skillet until crisp, then remove and reserve.
Saute the onion and garlic in the drippings, then add the shrimp and Creole seasoning and cook until just pink.
Pour in the stock to make a light brown gravy and return the bacon and sausage to the pan.
Spoon the shrimp and gravy over the creamy grits and serve.

name: Chicken, Andouille and Shrimp Jambalaya
steps: Brown the andouille and chicken thighs in oil, then saute the onion, celery, bell pepper and garlic.
Toast the rice in the pot, then stir in the diced tomatoes.
Add the broth, thyme, bay leaves, paprika and cayenne and bring to a boil.
Cover and simmer until the rice is tender and has absorbed the liquid.
Stir in the seasoned shrimp and cook a few minutes until pink, then garnish with green onion and parsley.

name: Chicken and Dumplings
steps: Simmer the whole chicken with celery, onion, chicken base, salt and pepper in about 12 cups water for 1 hour, then remove the chicken and strain the broth.
Mix flour with the ice water until shaggy, knead briefly, roll out thin, and cut into roughly 2-inch dumpling strips.
Shred the cooled chicken, discarding bones and skin.
Whisk the cream of chicken soup and a cornstarch slurry into the broth and bring to a rolling boil.
Drop in the dumplings one at a time, simmer about 5 minutes until tender, then stir the shredded chicken back in to heat through.

name: Pot Roast
steps: Season the chuck roast generously with salt and pepper and sear on all sides in hot oil.
Place the roast in the slow cooker with onion, garlic, thyme and beef broth.
Cook on low until tender, about 8 to 9 hours, adding the carrots and potatoes during the final 1.5 to 2 hours.
Remove the meat and vegetables, then whisk a cornstarch slurry into the cooking liquid and simmer briefly to make gravy.
Shred or slice the roast and serve with the vegetables and gravy.

name: Beef Stew
steps: Coat the beef chunks in salt, pepper and flour, then brown in batches in hot oil and butter and set aside.
Soften the onion in the same pot, add garlic, then pour in the red wine and scrape up the browned bits.
Return the beef, add broth, tomato paste, thyme, bay leaves and seasonings, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer 90 minutes.
Add the carrots, celery, potatoes and mushrooms; cover and simmer another hour until tender.
Stir in the frozen peas, adjust seasoning, and serve hot with parsley.

name: Meatloaf
steps: Heat the oven to 350 F.
Mix the ground beef, bread crumbs, onion, milk, egg, 2 tablespoons ketchup, Worcestershire, parsley and seasonings by hand until just combined.
Press the mixture into a loaf pan in an even layer.
Whisk the remaining ketchup, brown sugar and vinegar into a glaze and spread it over the top.
Bake uncovered about 55 minutes, then rest 8 to 10 minutes before slicing.

name: Chicken-Fried Steak with Cream Gravy
steps: Stir the salt, pepper, paprika and cayenne into the flour, and whisk the buttermilk with the eggs in a second bowl.
Dredge each cube steak in the seasoned flour, dip in the buttermilk-egg, then back into the flour for a thick crust.
Shallow-fry the steaks in about an inch of hot oil until golden and crisp on both sides, then drain.
Pour off all but a few tablespoons of the drippings, sprinkle in seasoned flour, and stir to brown into a roux.
Gradually whisk in the milk and simmer until thickened; season the cream gravy and spoon over the steaks.

name: Biscuits and Sausage Gravy
steps: Cook the pork sausage over medium heat, breaking it into crumbles, until no longer pink.
Add the butter to melt, then stir in the flour, salt and pepper and cook into a roux.
Gradually pour in the milk, stirring constantly, and bring to a boil.
Cook and stir until the gravy thickens, about 2 minutes, then adjust salt and plenty of black pepper.
Split warm biscuits and ladle the sausage gravy generously over the top.

name: Chana Masala (छोले)
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight, then pressure cook until tender and soft.
Heat oil and saute onions until golden, then add ginger garlic paste and chopped tomatoes with salt; cook until mushy.
Stir in turmeric, chili powder, coriander, cumin and garam masala; cook 3 to 4 minutes until aromatic.
Add cooked chickpeas with their stock plus water; cover and simmer about 15 minutes until thick.
Finish with dried fenugreek and mango powder, garnish with cilantro and serve with rice or roti.

name: Rajma (राजमा)
steps: Soak kidney beans overnight, then pressure cook until soft and creamy.
Heat oil, crackle cumin seeds, then saute onions until golden and add ginger garlic paste.
Add pureed tomatoes with turmeric, chili powder and coriander; cook until oil separates.
Add the boiled beans with their water, mash a few for body, and simmer 15 to 20 minutes until thick.
Stir in garam masala, garnish with cilantro and serve with steamed rice (rajma chawal).

name: Dal Tadka (दाल तड़का)
steps: Pressure cook the lentil mix with water and turmeric until soft and mushy.
Saute cumin, ginger garlic, onions and spices in a little ghee, add tomatoes and cook until soft.
Stir the cooked lentils into the masala and simmer 6 to 7 minutes, adjusting consistency with water.
Make the tadka: heat ghee, fry cumin seeds, dried chilies and garlic, then add chili powder.
Pour the sizzling tadka over the dal, stir in fenugreek, garnish with cilantro and serve.

name: Saag Paneer (साग पनीर)
steps: Blanch the spinach briefly, refresh in cold water, then puree with green chilies.
Saute cumin seeds, onions and ginger garlic paste in oil until golden, then add tomato and spices.
Add the spinach puree, season with salt, and simmer a few minutes until cooked and thickened.
Stir in cubed paneer and a splash of cream, simmer gently so paneer softens in the sauce.
Finish with garam masala and serve hot with naan or roti.

name: Aloo Gobi (आलू गोभी)
steps: Heat oil, temper cumin seeds, then saute onions and ginger garlic paste.
Add tomato, turmeric, chili and coriander powder and cook into a thick masala.
Add potato and cauliflower florets, toss to coat, season with salt.
Cover and cook on low heat, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender but not mushy.
Sprinkle garam masala and dried mango powder, garnish with cilantro and serve with roti.

name: Chicken Tikka Masala (चिकन टिक्का मसाला)
steps: Marinate chicken in yogurt, ginger garlic paste, chili powder, turmeric and garam masala for a few hours.
Grill or pan-roast the chicken tikka until charred and just cooked, then set aside.
Saute onions in butter, add ginger garlic, tomato puree and ground spices; cook until oil separates.
Stir in cream and crushed dried fenugreek, then add the grilled chicken and simmer until the sauce thickens.
Adjust salt, finish with garam masala and serve with naan or rice.

name: Moambe Chicken
steps: Wash the chicken pieces and pat them dry, then brown both sides in batches in 4 tbsp peanut oil over high heat and set aside.
Add the remaining oil, reduce to medium low, and saute the chopped onions until golden.
Stir in tomato paste, tomato puree, green onions, garlic, pepper flakes, ginger, salt, and water and mix well.
Return the chicken, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on low for 18 to 20 minutes.
Remove a cup of sauce, stir it into the peanut butter, then return that mixture to the pot.
Cook uncovered for 10 more minutes until the chicken is fully cooked with no pink remaining, then serve.
source: The Cookbook of Authentic African Cuisine (Cairon Vickers)

name: Congolese Madesu Stew (Kidney Bean Stew)
steps: Soak the beans in cool water for 10 to 12 hours, then drain and discard the soaking water.
Boil the beans in 6 cups of fresh water with a clove-pricked onion and bay leaves, covered, for 50 minutes to an hour, then drain and reserve the beans and liquid separately.
Crush the remaining onion, garlic, and green onion, then saute that mixture with chili flakes in 1 to 2 tbsp oil.
Add the crushed tomatoes and cook 3 to 4 minutes, then pour in 3 cups reserved bean liquid and 2 cups vegetable broth.
Stir in the ketchup to balance the acidity, season with salt and pepper, then add the boiled beans.
Cook uncovered for 20 to 25 minutes until stew-like, stirring occasionally, then serve over rice if desired.
source: The Cookbook of Authentic African Cuisine (Cairon Vickers)

name: Congolese Egusi Soup
steps: Brown the stew meat in oil until browned but not cooked through, then transfer to a deep pot, cover with water, bring to a slow boil, and simmer.
In the same skillet, fry the chopped onion, chili pepper, and tomatoes until they begin to soften.
Add tomato sauce, dried shrimp, and ground egusi to the skillet, combine well, and heat through.
Add the egusi mixture to the meat pot along with more water and stir into a smooth soup.
Cook 15 to 20 minutes until meat and vegetables are done, then stir in the chopped spinach.
Season with salt and cayenne, cook until the greens are tender, and serve.
source: The Cookbook of Authentic African Cuisine (Cairon Vickers)

name: Moukalou (Fish and Chickpea Curry)
steps: Soften the ginger, onion, and garlic in 2 tbsp oil in a pan.
Add the diced sweet potato, bell pepper, curry powder, and pepper flakes and cook for a minute, then stir in the chickpeas and coconut milk.
Bring to a boil, then cover and gently simmer 18 to 20 minutes until the potatoes are tender, seasoning with salt and pepper.
In a separate skillet, cook the fish fillets in the remaining 2 tbsp oil.
Divide the chickpea mixture into shallow bowls and top each with a fish fillet.
Add lime wedges for squeezing and serve.
source: The Cookbook of Authentic African Cuisine (Cairon Vickers)

name: Congolese Eggplant Curry
steps: Heat the oil in a large pot and saute the chopped onions for several minutes.
Add the chili pepper, ginger, garlic, and curry powder and cook on medium heat 2 to 3 minutes, stirring often.
Add the potatoes and cook until they start to soften, then add the eggplant and fry until it begins to brown.
Reduce the heat and simmer 8 to 10 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste and tomatoes and simmer until the sauce thickens and everything is tender.
Remove from the heat, stir in the coconut milk, and serve.
source: The Cookbook of Authentic African Cuisine (Cairon Vickers)

name: Plantains and Coconut Milk
steps: Peel and slice the plantains.
Combine all the ingredients except the coconut milk in a pan and stir gently while slowly heating.
When the plantains have heated and start to soften, add the coconut milk a bit at a time, stirring so it gets absorbed.
Simmer until the plantains are tender, then serve.
source: The Cookbook of Authentic African Cuisine (Cairon Vickers)

name: Congo Coupe Coupe (Grilled Marinated Beef)
steps: Mix the Maggi sauce, cayenne, oil, and salt together to create the marinade.
Pour the marinade over the beef in a glass casserole dish.
Marinate in the refrigerator for a half hour to an hour.
Cook the beef slowly on an outdoor grill over low heat.
Baste the beef with marinade every 20 to 30 minutes and cook for 2 to 5 hours depending on thickness.
Check while cooking until the beef is done as you like it, then serve.
source: The Cookbook of Authentic African Cuisine (Cairon Vickers)

name: Mayebo in Peanut Sauce
steps: Soak the dried mushrooms in water for 4 to 8 hours, then rinse them well.
Place the mushrooms in a pan with a bit of water and simmer for 8 to 10 minutes.
Dissolve the peanut paste in 2 glasses of water.
Heat the palm oil in a frying pan and brown the spices, then pour the mixture into the main pan.
Add the remaining ingredients and salt as desired while it cooks.
Grate the nutmeg over the top, stir, cook a few minutes longer, and serve.
source: The Cookbook of Authentic African Cuisine (Cairon Vickers)

name: Congolese Cumin Sauced Beef
steps: Bring the meat to a boil in 1 inch of water over high heat in a large pot, then cover and lower the heat to medium.
As the meat cooks, make the sauce by combining the rest of the ingredients in a separate large pot.
Bring the sauce to a slow boil, then turn off the heat.
Add the sauce pot to the meat pot.
Simmer until the meat is done and for another half hour.
Serve warm.
source: The Cookbook of Authentic African Cuisine (Cairon Vickers)

name: Liboke (Fish Baked in Banana Leaves)
steps: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees, then make 3 or 4 diagonal cuts through each side of the fish, season with salt and pepper, squeeze lemon over it, and set aside.
Cut the banana leaves into large rectangles, submerge in water, clean, and pat dry (use foil if you do not have leaves).
Heat the leaves over high heat to wilt them and make them flexible.
Place the fish on a leaf, season with garlic and ginger, then add tomatoes and onions.
Fold the leaves over with inward pressure so no juice runs out, double seal the sides, and tie or wrap the bundle.
Bake at 375 degrees for 12 to 20 minutes, let cool, open a packet to check doneness, and serve.
source: The Cookbook of Authentic African Cuisine (Cairon Vickers)

name: Air Fryer Latkes
steps: Scrub the potatoes, grate them in a food processor, and set the shreds in a bowl of cool water.
Grate the onion, then squeeze out the liquid in a towel.
Whisk the eggs, then add the salt, pepper, matzo meal, 1 tablespoon potato starch, baking powder, and grated onion. Drain the potatoes and fold them in.
Form the mixture into flat circles, dip in dry potato starch if you like, and spray one side with oil.
Place oil side down in the air fryer, spray the tops, and cook at 380F for 9 minutes, turning after 5 minutes. Serve.
source: The Complete Air Fryer Cookbook (Shon Miles)

name: Avocado Egg Boat
steps: Slice the avocado in half and remove the pit. Scoop out a little flesh from the middle to make room, leaving some on the shell.
Chop the chives and parsley and use them to season the avocado boats along with salt and pepper.
Crack an egg into each avocado half.
Cook in the air fryer for 6 minutes at 350F.
Sprinkle with extra chives and parsley to serve.
source: The Complete Air Fryer Cookbook (Shon Miles)

name: Sriracha-Honey Chicken Wings
steps: Preheat the air fryer to 360F. Add the wings and cook for 30 minutes, turning them about every 7 minutes so they brown evenly.
While the wings cook, add the honey, sriracha, soy sauce, butter, and lime juice to a small saucepan and bring to a boil for about 3 minutes.
When the wings are done, toss them in a bowl with the sauce until fully coated.
Sprinkle with cilantro and serve right away.
source: The Complete Air Fryer Cookbook (Shon Miles)

name: Korean Chicken Wings
steps: Preheat the air fryer to 400F. Line a pan with foil and set a rack inside.
Season the wings with salt and pepper, place them on the rack, and cook for 20 minutes, turning once halfway through.
While they cook, stir together the gochujang, mayonnaise, agave, sesame oil, ginger, garlic, and sugar and let it sit.
When the wings hit about 160F inside, move them to a bowl and toss with half the sauce.
Return the wings to the air fryer for another 5 minutes until the sauce glazes over, then sprinkle with sesame seeds and green onions.
source: The Complete Air Fryer Cookbook (Shon Miles)

name: Coconut Chicken Tenders
steps: Rinse and pat the chicken tenders dry. Set up three bowls: flour with salt, pepper, ginger, and onion powder; egg with coconut milk; and panko with coconut flakes.
Dip each tender into the flour mix, then the egg mix, then the panko mix, coating fully at each step.
Spray the preheated basket with oil, lay the chicken in a single layer, and spray the tops lightly.
Air fry at 360F for 10 minutes, flipping once halfway through and spraying with oil again. Serve.
source: The Complete Air Fryer Cookbook (Shon Miles)

name: Breaded Pork Chops
steps: Preheat the air fryer.
Coat the pork chops thickly with Dijon mustard.
In a shallow bowl, combine the breadcrumbs, salt, pepper, and cayenne, then dredge the chops in the crumbs.
Arrange the chops on a rack set over a sheet pan and spritz the coating with a little oil spray.
Cook until the coating is brown, about 5 minutes on each side.
source: The Complete Air Fryer Cookbook (Shon Miles)

name: Pork Satay with Peanut Sauce
steps: Mix half the garlic with the ginger, 1 teaspoon chili paste, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, and 1 tablespoon oil. Toss the cubed pork in it and marinate 15 minutes.
Preheat the air fryer to 392F. Put the marinated pork in the basket and roast for 12 minutes, turning once, until browned and cooked.
Meanwhile, heat 1 tablespoon oil in a saucepan and gently saute the shallot with the rest of the garlic, then add the coriander and fry briefly.
Stir in the coconut milk, peanuts, the remaining chili paste, and 1 tablespoon soy sauce, then gently boil 5 minutes, adding water if it gets too thick.
Season the sauce to taste and serve it with the pork satay.
source: The Complete Air Fryer Cookbook (Shon Miles)

name: 3-Ingredient Fried Catfish
steps: Preheat the air fryer to 400F.
Rinse the catfish and pat dry. Pour the fish fry seasoning into a large ziploc bag, add the fillets one at a time, seal, and shake to coat fully.
Spray olive oil over each fillet and place them in the basket. Cook for 10 minutes, then flip and cook 10 minutes more.
Flip again and cook another 2 to 3 minutes until your desired crispness, then serve.
source: The Complete Air Fryer Cookbook (Shon Miles)

name: Shrimp with Lemon and Chili
steps: Preheat the air fryer to 400F.
Toss the shrimp with the lemon slices and olive oil, then add the garlic powder and a pinch each of salt and pepper.
Place the shrimp and lemon slices in the basket and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the chili slices, shake to toss, and cook about 2 minutes more until the shrimp are opaque.
Transfer to a bowl, sprinkle with flaky sea salt, and serve hot.
source: The Complete Air Fryer Cookbook (Shon Miles)

name: Tater Tots in Bacon
steps: Wrap the tater tots in bacon and place them in the basket, taking care not to overcrowd it.
Cook for 8 minutes at 400F.
Transfer to a plate and top with the shredded cheese and scallions.
Serve with sour cream.
source: The Complete Air Fryer Cookbook (Shon Miles)

name: Mass Building Sweet Potato Pancakes
steps: Pierce the sweet potato and microwave until soft (about 8 minutes), then cool and remove the skin.
Blend the oats into a fine powder and set aside in a bowl.
Blend the sweet potato until smooth and mix it with the powdered oats.
Stir in the egg, egg whites, vanilla, cinnamon, honey and yogurt to form a batter.
Cook roughly a quarter of the batter in a sprayed pan over medium heat for 1 to 2 minutes per side, then top with the berries and repeat.

name: Banana and Almond Muscle Oatmeal
steps: Stir the oats and milk together in a large bowl and microwave for 2 minutes.
Mix in the diced banana, peanut butter, whey protein and chopped almonds.
Serve right away while warm.

name: Turkey Muscle Omelette
steps: Crack the eggs into a bowl and whisk.
Heat half the oil over medium heat and cook the turkey, kale and cheese for 5 to 6 minutes.
In a second pan, heat the rest of the oil and cook the eggs for about 4 minutes.
Add the turkey mix to the eggs, scatter baby spinach on top, then fold the omelette in half.
Cook for another 2 to 3 minutes, then plate up and serve.

name: Brawny Chicken and Chorizo Jambalaya
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat and brown the chicken for about 8 minutes, then set aside.
Fry the onion until tender, then add the garlic, chorizo, Cajun seasoning and red pepper and cook for about 5 minutes.
Stir in the brown rice, chopped tomatoes, chicken and chicken stock.
Cover and simmer for around 25 minutes until the rice is soft.

name: Muscle Moroccan Chicken Casserole
steps: Season the chicken with salt, pepper, paprika and ground cumin.
Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat and cook the chicken with the onion for about 4 minutes per side.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes plus 200ml of water and add the honey, harissa, courgettes and chickpeas, stirring to combine.
Bring to a simmer and cook for around 15 minutes, then plate up and serve.

name: Mighty Mexican Chicken Stew
steps: Heat the olive oil in a deep pan over medium heat and cook the onion and peppers for 2 to 3 minutes.
Stir in the chipotle paste and chopped tomatoes, then add the chicken with just enough water to cover it by 1cm and simmer for about 20 minutes until cooked through.
In a separate pan, cook the quinoa in stock for about 12 minutes, then add the pinto beans and cook 3 minutes more.
Drain the quinoa, stir in the coriander and lime juice, then serve the chicken over the quinoa topped with the tomato sauce.

name: Tasty Beef and Broccoli Stir Fry
steps: Heat the olive oil in a frying pan over medium to high heat.
Season the diced beef with salt and pepper and stir-fry for 2 minutes until browned, then set aside.
Add the broccoli and celery and fry for a further 2 minutes.
Pour in the beef stock, cover, and simmer the veg for 2 minutes.
Return the beef to the pan, toss for another minute, then serve with the horseradish sauce.

name: Bulk-Up Lamb Curry and Peanut Stew
steps: Preheat the oven to 190C (375F).
In a large casserole dish over medium heat, fry 2 tbsp of coconut cream with the curry paste for about a minute, then add the lamb and brown.
Stir in the rest of the coconut cream with a cup of water, plus the potatoes, onion, cinnamon stick, tamarind, fish sauce and peanuts.
Reduce to a simmer, cover, and transfer to the oven for 2 hours until the lamb is tender.
Scatter the sliced chilli over the top and serve.

name: Lemony Salmon
steps: Preheat the grill, then mix the garlic, tarragon, salt, pepper, olive oil and the lemon juice and zest in a bowl.
Coat the salmon fillets in the marinade, cover and leave for 10 minutes.
Place the fillets on a tray and pour the marinade over the top.
Grill for around 10 minutes until cooked through, then serve with the rocket.

name: Mighty Tuna Melts
steps: Toast the bread and preheat the grill.
Mix the spring onions, mayonnaise, tuna, chilli flakes, salt, pepper and lemon juice in a bowl.
Spread the tuna mix over the toast and sprinkle the grated cheese on top.
Grill until the cheese bubbles, then plate up and serve.

name: Chocolate Peanut Protein Shake
steps: Add all the ingredients to a blender.
Blend until smooth.
Pour into a glass and enjoy.

name: Jason's Peanut Protein Bars
steps: Whisk the coconut cream until smooth, then mix in the protein powder and almond butter.
Stir in the rolled oats until thoroughly combined.
Press the mixture into a baking tray and smooth the surface.
Chill in the fridge for around 8 hours, then cut into 12 bars.

name: Beef and Broccoli with Garlic Sauce
steps: Cut the beef lengthwise into 2-inch strips, then crosswise into thin slices, toss with a little oil, salt, and white pepper, then cover and refrigerate 30 minutes.
Cut the broccoli into florets and stems, blanch in boiling water for 2 minutes, drain, and rinse under cold water.
Mix the cornstarch, sesame oil, and broth into a smooth slurry and set aside.
Heat a wok until very hot, add oil, and stir-fry the beef for about 2 minutes until browned, then remove.
Stir-fry the garlic, ginger, and bean sauce for 30 seconds, add the bamboo shoots for 1 minute, then return the beef and broccoli.
Stir in the cornstarch slurry and cook 15 seconds until thickened, then serve.

name: Beef Lo Mein
steps: Mix the beef with 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 1 tablespoon rice wine, and the cornstarch, then let stand 20 minutes.
Combine the remaining soy sauce and rice wine with the oyster sauce and sugar, then set aside.
Boil the noodles for about 2 minutes until al dente, drain, rinse with cold water, and toss with the sesame oil.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a wok, stir-fry the carrots 1 minute, add the shredded cabbage and mushrooms for 1 minute more, then remove.
Heat more oil and stir-fry the beef until browned, about 2 minutes, then remove.
Wipe the wok, heat the remaining oil, stir-fry the noodles until heated through, then stir in the vegetables, beef, and sauce to mix well.

name: Scallops in Black Bean Sauce
steps: Heat the oil in a preheated wok, add the garlic and stir, then add the ginger and stir-fry together for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Mix in the rinsed and lightly mashed black beans, then toss in the scallops and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add the light soy sauce, rice wine, sugar, and chiles.
Lower the heat and simmer for 2 minutes, adding a little stock if needed.
Finally add the scallion, then stir and serve.

name: Sweet and Sour Prawns
steps: Marinate the prawns for 20 minutes in egg white, cornstarch, salt, water, and oil.
Mix together the tomato ketchup, sambal oelek, sugar, rice wine, and cornstarch into a sauce.
Peel and finely slice the garlic and ginger, and finely slice the scallions.
Heat oil in a wok and fry half the prawns for about 45 seconds, then drain.
In a clean wok, fry the garlic and ginger for 30 seconds, add the prawns, and stir constantly.
Add the prepared sauce, fry for 1 minute, then stir in the chopped scallions and serve.

name: Barbecued Pork (Cha Siu)
steps: Cut the meat into pieces about 5 inches by 2 inches by 1 inch.
Sprinkle the meat with all the remaining ingredients except the water, mix well, and marinate overnight or for at least 5 hours in the refrigerator.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F.
Add 1 cup water to a roasting pan and set a rack on top.
Place the pork on the rack and roast for 30 minutes on each side, total cooking time 1 hour, basting 3 or 4 times. Do not cover.

name: Cashew Chicken
steps: Bone and skin the chicken, then cut into 1-inch cubes.
Heat a small amount of oil in a skillet, roll the chicken in cornstarch, and cook until light tan, then remove.
Saute the chopped scallions and sliced mushrooms, then add the water chestnuts with their liquid.
Add the chicken, the soy sauce, and water, then bring to a boil and thicken with cornstarch.
Add the cashew nuts and serve over cooked rice.

name: Almond Chicken
steps: Dice the chicken into 1-inch cubes, combine the marinade ingredients (salt, white pepper, cornstarch, soy sauce, egg white), add the chicken, mix well, and let stand 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a wok, add the chicken, and stir-fry until browned, then remove and drain well.
Stir-fry the ginger, onion, pepper, and bamboo shoots for about 1 minute until crisp-tender.
Combine the seasoning sauce ingredients (rice vinegar, soy sauce, salt, sugar, cornstarch) in a bowl, add to the wok, and bring to a boil.
Return the chicken to the boiling sauce and stir-fry until coated, then add the almonds, mix well, and serve hot.

name: Mixed Seafood Curry
steps: Heat the oil in a large wok and stir-fry the shallots, galangal, and garlic for 1 to 2 minutes until they start to soften.
Add the coconut milk, lemongrass, fish sauce, and chili sauce, bring to a boil, reduce the heat, and let simmer for 1 to 2 minutes.
Add the prepared shrimp, squid, salmon, and tuna, and let simmer for 3 to 4 minutes until the shrimp have turned pink and the fish is cooked.
Add the mussels and cover with a lid, letting simmer for 1 to 2 minutes until they have opened.
Discard any mussels that remain closed, garnish with Chinese chives, and serve immediately with rice.

name: Hoisin Pork with Garlic Noodles
steps: Boil the noodles for 3 minutes until soft, drain well, rinse under cold water, drain again, and set aside.
Sprinkle the pork slices with the sugar and toss together, then heat a wok over high heat, add the oil, add the pork, and stir-fry for about 3 minutes until cooked through.
Remove the pork and keep warm, then add both vinegars to the wok and boil until reduced to about 5 tablespoons.
Pour in the hoisin sauce with the scallions, let bubble until reduced by half, then add to the pork and stir together.
Wipe the wok, reheat with the garlic oil, fry the garlic slices for about 30 seconds until golden and crisp, then remove.
Add the noodles to the wok and stir to warm, divide between plates, top with the pork and onion mixture, and sprinkle with garlic slices and cilantro.

name: Mu Shu Pork
steps: Cook the pork completely in 2 tablespoons oil.
Add the garlic, salt, pepper, and shredded ginger.
Add hoisin sauce until the mixture is coated.
Spoon the meat and hoisin sauce onto the edge of a flour tortilla and sprinkle chopped scallions on top.
Roll up the tortilla and serve with a small dish of hoisin sauce for dipping.

name: Pork Dumplings (Gyoza)
steps: Heat the sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and saute the cabbage, onion, garlic, and carrot until the cabbage is tender and translucent.
Add the pork and beaten egg, stirring constantly, and cook until the pork is browned, then remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Place about a tablespoon of filling in the center of each wrapper, fold in half, moisten the inner edges, and pinch to seal.
Preheat the vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and fry the gyoza until lightly browned, about 1 minute per side.
Add the water, cover the skillet, and let the dumplings steam until almost all the liquid has evaporated.
Serve with soy dipping sauce or spicy soy dipping sauce.

name: Chicken Teriyaki
steps: Mix the teriyaki sauce ingredients (soy sauce, brown sugar, vinegar, ginger, garlic) together in a bowl and set aside.
Pound the chicken breasts to half-inch thickness, cut each in half, and season with freshly ground pepper.
Heat the oil in a large pan over medium-high heat and cook the chicken until golden brown, about 3 to 4 minutes per side.
Pour the teriyaki sauce over the chicken without stirring and let it absorb the sauce while cooking, flipping as needed until no pink remains.
Transfer the chicken to a dish, then thicken the pan sauce with a cornstarch and water slurry, whisking about 30 seconds until thickened.
Pour the sauce over the chicken and serve with steamed rice.

name: Pork Bone Ramen (Tonkotsu Ramen)
steps: Prepare the ramen noodles according to the package instructions and place a single serving in a ramen bowl.
Combine the seasoning sauce ingredients (soy sauce, dashi, sugar, mirin) in a small bowl.
Heat the basic ramen soup stock to boiling and stir in the seasoning sauce, adjusting the taste with more sauce, salt, or broth as desired.
Pour the hot seasoned stock over the noodles.
Top with chasu, half a marinated runny yolk egg, and chopped green onions.
Drizzle with mayu (black garlic oil) and serve.

name: Stir-Fried Udon Noodles (Yaki Udon)
steps: Prepare the udon noodles according to the package instructions, boiling to loosen them, then rinse and drain.
Heat the oil in a wok over medium heat and saute the pork belly until browned.
Add the onion and cook until translucent, then add the cabbage, carrot, shiitake, and green onion and cook until the vegetables are wilted.
Add the noodles, mentsuyu, soy sauce, and black pepper, and mix well, adjusting the flavor with more seasoning as needed.
Transfer to a serving dish and top with bonito flakes and chopped green onion.

name: Breaded Pork Cutlet Rice Bowl (Katsudon)
steps: Whisk together the sauce ingredients (dashi, sugar, soy sauce, mirin) in a bowl and set aside.
Season the pork chops with salt and pepper, dust with flour, dip in beaten egg, then coat in panko, pressing to make the crumbs stick.
Fry the breaded pork in hot oil until golden brown, about 5 minutes per side, then drain and cut into half-inch strips.
In another skillet, saute the sliced onions until slightly caramelized, pour in the sauce, and bring to a boil.
Arrange the cutlets over the onions, reduce heat to low, and pour the beaten eggs over the meat without stirring until the egg sets.
Place the cutlets with sauce, onions, and egg over hot rice and garnish with green onion.

name: Shrimp Tempura
steps: Whisk the batter ingredients (flour, cornstarch, baking powder, salt, ice-cold water) in a bowl and keep refrigerated until use.
Make a diagonal slit on the undersides of the shrimp to straighten them, leaving the tails intact.
Dip each shrimp into the cold batter, then into the breadcrumbs, packing the crumbs well onto the surface.
Heat oil in a wok or fryer at least 2 inches deep, then dip the shrimp in batter and breadcrumbs a second time.
Drop the shrimp into the hot oil one by one without crowding and cook until golden brown, about 3 minutes.
Drain on paper towels and serve with tempura or mentsuyu sauce for dipping.

name: Japanese-Style Chicken Wings (Tebasaki)
steps: Combine the basting sauce ingredients (soy sauce, water, sugar, vinegar, salt) in a bowl and set aside.
Mix the flour, white pepper, garlic powder, and cayenne together, then dip the wings into the beaten eggs and then into the flour mixture.
Preheat the oven to 350F and heat the butter and oil for frying in a heavy skillet over medium heat.
Fry the wings until deep brown and crisp, then transfer them to a shallow roasting pan in a single layer.
Bake the wings about 30 minutes, basting with the sauce and turning them over from time to time.
Serve garnished with chopped green onions.

name: Takeout Miso Soup with Tofu and Wakame
steps: Combine the kombu and 6 cups water in a pot and bring to a simmer, removing the kombu once it rises to the surface.
Add the katsuobushi, simmer for 10 seconds, skim off any foam, then strain the dashi through cheesecloth into a clean pot.
Soak the wakame in cold water for 5 minutes, then drain and set aside.
Bring 4 cups of the dashi to a boil, reduce the heat, and stir in the miso paste.
Add the tofu, bring to a simmer, then pull from the heat and add the wakame and green onion.
Serve hot.

name: Miso Salmon Cakes
steps: Heat the oils in a skillet, add the garlic and green onion, and cook until tender, then stir in the sake and miso paste and bring to a simmer.
Stir in the cream and lime juice, return to a simmer, and cook about 8 minutes until the sauce thickens, then set aside.
In a bowl, combine the breadcrumbs, flaked salmon, cilantro, and onion.
Whisk the water, soy sauce, and egg together, then stir half of it into the salmon mixture.
Heat 2 tablespoons vegetable oil in a skillet, form the salmon mixture into 4-inch patties, and fry until golden brown, about 4 minutes per side.
Drizzle the miso cream sauce over the cakes and serve.

name: Chicken and Vegetable Miso Soup
steps: Heat the oil in a pot, add the onion, mushrooms, and celery, season with salt and pepper, and cook 5 to 7 minutes until the vegetables soften and brown.
Add the miso, broth, and chicken breasts and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and cook partially covered for 15 to 20 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through.
Remove the chicken, shred it with two forks, and return it to the pot.
Add the bok choy, cover, and cook about 3 minutes until the bok choy wilts.
Serve hot.

name: Baked Cod with Miso
steps: Preheat the oven to 350F.
In a saucepan, add the miso and mirin and cook over low heat until the miso dissolves.
Allow the mixture to cool, then pour it into a dish.
Dip the cod fillets in the mirin mixture and let them marinate for half an hour.
Transfer to the oven and bake for 10 minutes.
Serve warm.

name: Kimchi Miso Stew
steps: Heat the oil in a pan and cook the scallion whites with garlic and ginger, stirring frequently, for 3 minutes.
Stir in the broth, radish, soy sauce, and chili paste.
Simmer the radish mixture for 20 minutes.
Stir in the kimchi and tofu and bring back to a simmer.
Serve in bowls and top with the reserved dark green scallions.

name: Almond Flour Chocolate Chip Pancakes
steps: In a medium bowl, whisk the eggs with the almond milk and vanilla.
Add the maple syrup, salt, baking powder, almond meal, and rice flour, then whisk until just combined and stir in the chocolate chips (do not overmix).
Heat a nonstick griddle to medium heat (about 250 degrees F).
Pour 1/4 cup of batter per pancake and cook until bubbles form, then gently flip and cook until golden.
Serve with your favorite toppings.

name: Oatmeal and Apple Muffins
steps: Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F.
In a large bowl, stir together the egg, half and half, canola oil, and Splenda.
In a separate bowl, mix the chopped apple, walnuts, oats, salt, baking powder, nutmeg, and cinnamon.
Add the egg mixture to the apple mixture and stir until just combined, then pour the batter into a muffin pan.
Bake for 15 to 20 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool before serving.

name: Chicken Curry
steps: In a food processor, blend the red onion, ginger, garlic, and serrano pepper until coarse.
In a large pan over medium heat, cook the onion mixture for 5 to 8 minutes until browned.
Add the coriander, cumin, turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, and salt, then cook for 30 seconds.
Stir in the pureed tomato and yogurt, reduce heat to low, then add the chicken and toss to coat and cook for 2 minutes.
Add 1 cup of water, bring to a boil, then simmer 15 to 20 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Serve with a garnish of chopped cilantro.

name: Chicken and Coconut Stew
steps: Stir together the orange juice, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and cayenne to make the spice mixture and set aside.
In a Dutch oven over medium heat, cook the onion in 2 tablespoons coconut oil for 5 minutes, then add the ginger and garlic and cook 2 minutes more.
Stir in the spice mixture and cook 1 minute, then transfer to a bowl and set aside.
Add the tomatoes and coconut milk to the pot, cook 2 to 3 minutes, then add the broth and a dash of salt and simmer 10 minutes, then add the sweet potatoes and cook 20 minutes until soft.
In a separate pan, brown the chicken in the remaining coconut oil for 8 to 10 minutes, then add it back to the pot along with the reserved spice mixture and cook 1 to 2 minutes.
Serve with a garnish of chopped cashews and unsweetened coconut flakes.

name: Thirty Minute Turkey Chili
steps: In a Dutch oven over medium heat, brown the ground turkey in 2 tablespoons olive oil for about 5 minutes.
Add the onion, bell pepper, and remaining olive oil and cook 5 to 8 minutes until soft, then stir in the garlic, salt, and pepper and cook 1 minute more.
Add the kidney beans, tomatoes, tomato sauce, water, green chiles, cumin, chili powder, smoked paprika, and bay leaves.
Bring to a boil and cook 10 to 15 minutes until thick, then stir in the Splenda and adjust the seasoning.
Serve with a garnish of sour cream and sliced green onions.

name: Maple Glazed Salmon
steps: Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F and spray a baking dish with cooking spray.
Whisk together the grapeseed oil, maple syrup, vinegar, soy sauce, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Place the salmon steaks in the dish and pour the sauce over the top, flipping a few times so both sides soak.
Sprinkle the bread crumbs over the fish, patting them to stick.
Bake for about 15 minutes (check at 10 minutes) until the fish flakes with a fork.

name: Lentil Lemon Cakes
steps: In a medium bowl, mix all the ingredients except the olive oil until well combined (soak the lentils at least 12 hours first).
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Divide the lentil mix into 8 parts, roll each into a ball, and flatten into a disk about 1/2 inch thick.
Cook each cake 3 to 4 minutes per side until golden, then serve hot.

name: Mexican Quinoa
steps: In a saucepan, combine 1 tablespoon olive oil, quinoa, garlic, and water, then cover and simmer 15 minutes until the water is absorbed.
Make the dressing by combining the lime juice and zest, 2 tablespoons olive oil, cumin, salt, and lemon pepper.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked quinoa, beans, avocado, tomato, corn, onion, spinach, and cilantro.
Toss with the dressing and serve warm.

name: Salted Caramel Chocolate Chip Cookies
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
In a blender, blend the soaked dates, sea salt, and water until smooth.
In a food processor, pulse the cashew butter, ground vanilla beans, and baking powder until smooth.
In a large bowl, stir together the cashew butter mixture, date mixture, and dark chocolate chips until just mixed, then drop spoonfuls onto a baking sheet.
Bake for 10 to 12 minutes, then cool on a wire rack before serving.

name: Cinnamon Almond Energy Balls
steps: In a medium bowl, stir together the almond butter and maple syrup until smooth.
Add the flaxseed, vanilla, cinnamon, and salt, then mix to combine.
Add the protein powder and fully incorporate (add another teaspoon of protein powder if the dough is too oily), then mix in the oats.
Roll into small balls and enjoy right away or store covered in the refrigerator to firm up.
